# Lattix Frontend Instructions

Apply these rules when editing UI, client apps, frontend state, API clients, forms, routing, or browser-side code.

UX and correctness:
- Preserve existing behavior unless explicitly changed.
- Prefer small, accessible, responsive UI changes.
- Keep components focused and composable.
- Separate presentation, state, API calls, and domain transformations.
- Avoid duplicating business rules that belong on the server unless needed for UX validation.

Security/privacy:
- Never expose secrets, tokens, internal keys, or unsafe config in client code.
- Do not log PII, auth headers, session IDs, tokens, or sensitive payloads.
- Treat all client input and server responses as untrusted.
- Escape/sanitize user-rendered content; avoid unsafe HTML injection.
- Use secure auth/session patterns already present in the project.
- Avoid leaking internal error details to users.

Accessibility:
- Use semantic HTML where possible.
- Ensure keyboard navigation, visible focus, labels, roles, and ARIA only when needed.
- Preserve or improve screen-reader behavior.
- Respect contrast and reduced-motion preferences.

Performance:
- Avoid unnecessary re-renders, large bundles, blocking work, and unbounded client memory.
- Lazy-load heavy routes/components where appropriate.
- Debounce expensive user-triggered work.
- Handle loading, empty, error, and retry states.

Testing:
- Add or update tests for user-visible behavior.
- Prefer interaction tests over implementation-detail snapshots.
- Cover accessibility-critical behavior, form validation, error states, and API boundary behavior.
- Do not weaken existing tests.

Observability:
- Use existing client telemetry patterns.
- Capture stable error codes and operation names when relevant.
- Do not emit sensitive data in analytics, logs, traces, or session replay.
